title: Truth Detectives: AI vs. Fake News

modules.title: The Story That Fooled Everyone
modules.sub: Dive into a viral news crisis and spot the first cracks
modules.description: Learners are dropped into a breaking news scenario where a shocking AI-generated story is spreading across social media. They make their first judgment calls about what feels off, setting the stage for the investigation ahead.

modules.title: How AI Writes Convincing Lies
modules.sub: Discover how AI generates text that sounds totally believable
modules.description: Learners explore the mechanics behind AI language models and how they produce fluent, confident-sounding content — even when it's completely false. They experiment with spotting AI writing patterns and begin building their personal detection checklist.

modules.title: Deepfakes: Real or Not?
modules.sub: Train your eye to catch manipulated images and videos
modules.description: Learners examine deepfake images and videos, learning the telltale visual and audio clues that give them away. They complete a series of real-or-fake challenges, adding new checklist items based on what they discover.

modules.title: The Clues Real Detectives Use
modules.sub: Borrow fact-checking techniques from professional investigators
modules.description: Learners study the methods that journalists and fact-checkers use, from reverse image searches to source verification. They apply these tools to evidence from the ongoing news crisis and sharpen their detection checklist into a reliable toolkit.

modules.title: You Decide: Publish or Delete?
modules.sub: Make a high-stakes editorial call with real story consequences
modules.description: Using their completed checklist, learners analyse the original viral story and decide whether it should be published, corrected, or removed. Their choice triggers different story outcomes, making the stakes of getting it wrong feel real and meaningful.

modules.title: Broadcast Your Truth Verdict
modules.sub: Present your verdict and defend it with evidence
modules.description: Learners craft and deliver a short 'truth verdict' explaining what the story was, how it was made, and why their judgment is correct. They share their reasoning with peers and reflect on how their checklist could protect others from being fooled.

synopsis: Step into a breaking news crisis where AI-generated stories are spreading fast â and you're the only one who can figure out what's real. Through choices that have real consequences inside the story, you'll learn exactly how AI creates and spreads misinformation, and build the skills to stop it.
rationale: Pre-teens are among the heaviest sharers of viral content yet receive almost no structured education on how AI systems generate and amplify misinformation at scale.
learning_outcome: Students can identify AI-generated misinformation using a self-built checklist, and make and defend a reasoned judgment about whether a piece of content is trustworthy.
